vBRIEFInfo.description: Bilingual edge reader in roadmap_render + spec_render (#435) + migrate_vbrief (#436). Canonical convention is schema-aligned from/to. Legacy source/target continues to work for at least one release cycle.

plan.title: v0.20.0 GA blocker: align plan.edges[] field names (from/to vs source/target)

narratives.Problem: Schema requires {from,to,type}; speckit doc example uses {from,to,type}; code (roadmap_render._build_edge_map) reads {source,target}; test fixtures use {source,target}. Silent dep-map failure when schema-compliant data meets code expecting legacy keys. #436 migrator and #435 aggregator both consume edges and will hit this in production.
narratives.Action: Update scripts/roadmap_render.py _build_edge_map to read both: edge.get("from") or edge.get("source",""); edge.get("to") or edge.get("target",""); prefers from/to when both present. Apply the same bilingual pattern in scripts/spec_render.py (#435 aggregator cross-scope topo sort) and scripts/migrate_vbrief.py (#436 speckit-plan translator). Update tests/cli/test_roadmap_render.py primary fixture to {from,to}; add legacy {source,target} regression fixture; add mixed-keys-within-one-plan fixture. Confirm strategies/speckit.md still uses {from,to}. Ensure vbrief/vbrief.md documents edges consistently.
narratives.Outcome: Edge reads never silently lose data regardless of which convention the input uses. #435 and #436 can be built on this reader without reintroducing the silent-empty-map failure.

items.title: Update roadmap_render.py _build_edge_map to read both conventions (from/to preferred)
Apply bilingual read pattern in spec_render.py aggregator (#435 touchpoint)
Apply bilingual read pattern in migrate_vbrief.py speckit-plan translator (#436 touchpoint)
Switch test_roadmap_render.py primary fixture to {from, to}
Confirm strategies/speckit.md edge example stays {from, to}
Ensure vbrief/vbrief.md edges documentation uses {from, to}
